creator: roam@orbitel.bg
text:
On Mon, Jun 18, 2001 at 02:38:29AM +0200, rguyom@pobox.com wrote:
> 
> >Number:         28245
> >Category:       ports
> >Synopsis:       bug in x11/xloadimage -tile option
> >Originator:     Rémi Guyomarch
> >Release:        FreeBSD 4.3-STABLE i386
> 	
> >How-To-Repeat:
> 	xloadimage -tile <some_image_file_smaller_in_x_and_y_than_your_screen>

As far as I can see, this is a fix to the xloadimage program itself,
not something specific to the FreeBSD port.  Could you try submitting
it to the xloadimage authors/maintainers for possible inclusion
in future xloadimage versions?

According to the xloadimage README file (the one in the distribution,
not the port README file ;), the author is Jim Frost at CenterLine
Software, reachable at <jimf@centerline.com>.

G'luck,
Peter

-- 
If the meanings of 'true' and 'false' were switched, then this sentence wouldn't be false.
